channel.title: Pasta on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Pasta on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Pasta Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup oil
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 2 ½ tbsp sugar
  • ¼ cup concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • ½ tsp basil
  • black pepper
  • salt

Instructions

Cook choice of pasta, drain and add the dressing. Add crushed feta cheese, parmesan cheese, grapes (red and green), red and green peppers, olives and celery.

Chill and serve.

item.title: Poor Man's Pasta
item.description:
Submitted by Derek Forgie

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • Macaroni
  • Cheese Dust

Instructions

Fill a metal container with a water-like substance. Find a way to make it hot enough for the water to make a boiling noise. Place several noodles into the noisy water.

Stir the noodles with a pointy or rounded stick until they become limpanized. Drain the leftover water into a plant that looks cold and thirsty.

item.title: Penné with Peppers, Mushrooms, and Sausage
item.description:
Submitted by Marleen and Bill Tregenza

Godparents of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 red bell peppers
  • 14 ozs fresh mushrooms
  • 8 ozs sausage, remove from casing
  • 2 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp oregano
  • 1 tbsp thyme
  • 2 garlic cloves
  • ½ cup fresh parsley
  • grated parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Cut peppers into ½ inch diagonal pieces. Place in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Drizzle with 1 tbsp of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste. Bake until charred (45 minutes), stir occasionally. Remove.

item.title: Penné with Italian Sausage, Tomato, and Herbs
item.description:
Submitted by Sue and Don Lloyd

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 4 hot or medium Italian sausages
  • 2 cups penné or corkscrew-shaped pasta
  • 1 tsp vegetable oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 3 gloves garlic, minced
  • 1-28 oz can of tomatoes, coarsely chopped
  • 2 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp dried oregano
  • ½ cup fresh parsley, chopped
  • pepper
  • 2 tbsp freshly grated parmesan cheese
  • 2-3 pinches of crushed red pepper

Instructions

In skillet, cook sausage over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes. Drain well. Cut into thin, round slices. Cook pasta until tender but firm. Drain and return to pot.

item.title: Chicken Tetrazzini
item.description:
Submitted by Christine Shank

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 boiling fowl (5 ½ - 6 lbs)
  • a few stalks of celery, chopped
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 1 quart milk
  • ¼ lb butter
  • 4 ozs hard flour
  • 2 cups chicken stock
  • 1 lb spaghetti
  • 1 lb mushrooms
  • ½ cup parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Boil fowl. Take meat from bones and put stalks of celery, onions and salt and pepper to taste into chicken stock. Simmer.

item.title: Angel Hair Pasta with Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Allison and Dave Rudback

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 2 boneless chicken breasts, cubed
  • 1 carrot, sliced diagonally
  • 1 cup broccoli florets
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 12 oz angel hair pasta
  • 2/3 cup chicken broth
  • 1/4 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1 tsp dried basil

Instructions

Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a medium sized skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towel.

item.title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Creamy Chicken & Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb boneless chicken breasts, cut up
  • 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend
  • 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup
  • 1/2 cup water

Instructions

Brown 1 lb cut-up boneless chicken breasts.

Add 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend, 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup and 1/2 cup water. Simmer until done.

Serves 4.

item.title: Olive Oil Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pound of spaghetti (I just guesstimated and used my own fettucini noodles)

  • 2 tbsp of salt

  • 1/4 cup + 2 tbps of extra-virgin olive oil

  • 8 garlic cloves, minced

  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes (less if you want it a bit less spicy - 1/4 tsp was good for the girls)

  • 2 tsp of lemon juice

  • 2 tsp of parsley

  • 1/2 tsp of salt (or more to taste)

item.title: Homemade Pasta/Noodles
item.description:
This is a mish-mash of a few different recipes for this I’ve seen on the web, along with a few different techniques. No single one had all the ingredients and info in the same spot, so I figured I’d just write it up myself. If it sucks, I’m sorry. :(

Ingredients

  • 2 cups of flour (I just use regular flour, but the internet nerds say to use semolina and some other weird stuff. Keep it simple!)
  • 2 eggs
  • 1/2 tsp olive oil
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup of water (just keep it handy - DO NOT just dump it in!! See the recipe below for the deets)

NOTE: This is a doubled-up recipes that makes around 4 good portions with enough left over for lunch the next day.

item.title: Chicken Bacon Ranch Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients:

  • 2 cups of shredded chicken (approximately 2 chicken breasts)
  • 2 tablespoons of dry ranch seasoning
  • 1 cup of pasta of choice (macaroni is good)
  • 3 1/2 cups of chicken or beef broth (beef gives more flavour)
  • 2 tablespoons of milk
  • 4 oz (half a brick) of cream cheese
  • 4-6 slices of bacon crumbled or sliced
  • 2 cups of shredded cheese of choice

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to450 degrees Fahrenheit and cook chicken breasts until fully cooked (about 15-20 minutes).
  2. Add shredded chicken and ranch seasoning into a bowl and combine.
  3. Add chicken, broth, and pasta into a large pot, stir, and bring to a boil.
  4. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and cover for 15-20 minutes until noodles are the preferred texture.

Note:Stir every so often to make sure noodles aren’t sticking to the bottom

item.title: Boursin One Dish Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 package of garlic and fine herbs Boursin cheese
  • 2 cups uncooked pasta (bowties work well)
  • 4 cloves of garlic finely chopped
  • 1/2 cup of cubed cheddar cheese
  • 2 cups of chopped fresh broccoli
  • 2 tablespoons of olive oil
  • 2 cups of stock (vegetable or chicken)
  • 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper
  • 1 teaspoon of salt
  • 3/4 shredded cheese (any kind)
  • 1/2 cup of heavy cream (can add extra cheese instead to thicken)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Add Boursin to middle of baking dish, surround with uncooked pasta, broccoli, and cheese cubes
  3. Add olive oil, spices, and stock over top of everything.
  4. Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes covered.
  5. Remove after 30 minutes. Add shredded cheese and cream, stir and place back in oven uncovered for 10 minutes or until pasta is tender.

If too runny, use the cornstarch and water method to thicken the sauce

item.title: Pasta Salad (Nandini)
item.description:
Ingredients

Dressing:

  • 1/4 cup oil
  • 1/4 cup vinegar
  • 2 1/2 tbsp sugar
  • 1/4 concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • 1/2 tsp basil
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Other stuff:

  • Whatever pasta you want
  • crushed feta cheese
  • grapes (red & green)
  • red and green peppers
  • olives
  • celery

Instructions

  1. Mix all that stuff up.  That’s your dressing.
  2. Cook choice of pasta, drain, and add the dressing.
  3. Add the other stuff cut up however you want, however much you want, because I don’t have these kinds of details.  You can blame Heather if it tastes bad.  She wrote down the initial recipe.
